recording_notes: Same phone-filming-RCA-monitor setup as V11. FLS skills lab visible — Stryker equipment, pegboard with needle drivers, peg boards, knot pushers. Wider framing shows more of the lab environment. Image quality still degraded by double-capture.

frame_analyses.description: At 6 seconds, both instruments in field near Penrose drain. Suture and needle visible. Needle loading already underway — extremely fast entry.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: FASTEST entry in the series. Instruments engaged with needle immediately.

frame_analyses.description: At 23 seconds, both instruments near drain working actively. Curved needle visible above drain in suture loop. Needle driving through second mark.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Fast, confident suture placement. Needle visible mid-drive through tissue.

frame_analyses.description: At 35 seconds, right instrument across upper portion of field. Suture visible through drain — both ends present. Suture placement completed, transitioning to first throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture placement completed in approximately 25 seconds — NEW FASTEST in the series (previous best V7: ~30s). This represents peak needle-driving efficiency.

frame_analyses.description: At 47 seconds, both instruments crossing near drain. Suture X pattern visible on drain. First throw wrapping/tightening in progress.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw completing. The dark suture cross pattern confirms accurate placement. Throw mechanics appear smooth and efficient.

frame_analyses.description: At 58 seconds, throw wrapping visible. Both instruments near drain with suture looped/crossed. Second throw in progress.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Already on second throw at 58 seconds — very fast progression. The throw phase is significantly accelerated compared to all previous videos.

frame_analyses.description: At 70 seconds, instruments working at drain level. Right instrument across field with suture. Third throw wrapping or early tightening.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Third throw at 70 seconds. All three throws will be completed by approximately 80-82 seconds — the fastest throw sequence in the series.

frame_analyses.description: At 87 seconds, suture tails extending upward from completed knot on drain. Instruments positioning for cutting. Cutting phase beginning.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cutting begins around 82-85 seconds. Knot completed — now entering the historically problematic cutting phase.

frame_analyses.description: At 99 seconds, suture tails visible extending upward. Instruments working on cutting. Cutting continuing.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cutting still in progress at 99 seconds. The cutting phase continues to be the longest individual phase.

frame_analyses.description: At 111 seconds, suture tails still visible. Instruments still in field cutting.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Extended cutting approaching 30 seconds. Improved from V10/V11 (50s each) but still the dominant time sink.

frame_analyses.description: At 115 seconds, instruments approaching withdrawal. Suture tails visible from completed knot. One instrument still near drain at lower right. Video ends at 116.6 seconds — task appears to complete at approximately 115 seconds. The knot is visible on the drain. Drain slit closure assessment limited by monitor recording quality but appears improved from V10/V11.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: NEW PERSONAL BEST TIME at approximately 115 seconds — 27 seconds faster than the previous best (V7: 142s). Every phase except cutting set new speed records. The drain appears reasonably closed despite monitor quality limitations.

knot_assessments.notes: Fast first throw completing by ~48s. Surgeon's knot wrapping pattern visible. Confidence low-moderate due to monitor quality.
Second throw at 58s — fastest second throw in the series at ~14 seconds. Hand switch difficult to confirm through monitor. Confidence low.
Third throw completed by ~82s. 20-second third throw — fast and controlled. Confidence low.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Monitor recording makes gap assessment unreliable. The drain edges appear approximated around the knot but detailed assessment is limited. Confidence very low.

technique_summary: V12 is a BREAKTHROUGH — the new personal best at approximately 115 seconds, obliterating the previous best of 142 seconds (V7) by 27 seconds. Every phase except cutting set new speed records: needle loading at 5 seconds (previous best: 10s), suture placement at 25 seconds (previous best: 30s), and the three-throw sequence completed in 49 seconds total (previous best: ~60s). The cutting phase at 33 seconds remains the longest individual phase but improved substantially from the 50-second times seen in V10/V11. This video demonstrates that the trainee's skill ceiling is significantly higher than V7/V9 suggested. The FLS score of ~479.5 would be the highest in the series. Image quality from the monitor recording limits confidence in penalty assessment, but the time improvement alone drives the score significantly higher.
improvement_suggestions: CELEBRATE: This is a 42% time improvement from baseline (198s → 115s) and a 19% improvement from previous best (142s → 115s)
Cutting at 33 seconds still represents 29% of total time — it's the only phase that didn't hit a new best. Targeted scissors drilling could push total time under 100 seconds
Consider recording directly from the trainer camera to enable more accurate penalty assessment — the time gains may be partially offset by undetectable placement/knot penalties at this image quality
If this was the first or second attempt in a fresh session, it validates the session-discipline recommendation — fresh performance is dramatically better
Next target: sub-100 seconds with direct camera recording for full scoring accuracy
strengths: NEW PERSONAL BEST TIME: 115 seconds (27s faster than V7)
NEW FASTEST throw sequence: 49 seconds total for all three throws
Cutting improved from 50s (V10/V11) to 33s — still room to improve but significant progress
All three throws completed efficiently in correct sequence
Drain not avulsed — tissue handling maintained at high speed
Task completed well within time limit

key_observations: NEW ALL-TIME BEST: 115s / 479.5 FLS — shatters previous best V7 (142s / 455.5)
Every phase set new speed record EXCEPT cutting (33s vs best of ~18s in post-2)
Needle loading: 5s (best), Suture placement: 25s (best), All throws: 49s (best)
Cutting improved from 50s (V10/V11) to 33s — still the dominant time sink at 29% of total
If performed as first attempt in fresh session: validates session discipline recommendation
NEXT TARGET: sub-100 seconds. Cutting drill could save 15-20 seconds
